account_signals.author: unknown
account_signals.text:
I think ranges/horizontal levels are the most important factor when looking at pairs.

This is because even as value of an asset changes, relative value between two assets should stay the same so long as nothing unique to one of those assets changes. In other words, asset pairs should always trade in a range until something changes that is specific to one or both parts of the pair unequally.

Let's do an example with cars and houses. If an entry level car is $30k, then likely the current entry l

The GOP primary heads to a May runoff between John Cornyn and Ken Paxton. In the November general, current polling averages and prediction markets (e.g., Kalshi) show the Republican nominee as the clear favorite to win, consistent with Texas's long Republican trend in statewide races. Democrats see it as competitive but trail in head-to-heads.

search_signals.author: unknown
search_signals.text:
This is the real split in prediction markets.

US regulated platforms have clear limits.
Offshore platforms do not.

That is not a small difference.
That is the whole story.

When a market crosses the line, regulated platforms have to stop.
Offshore exchanges keep going.

So this is not just a debate about one bad contract.
It is a fight over where trust stays, where money stays, and where this market is allowed to grow.

The side that keeps this business onshore will own the real advantage.

search_signals.author: unknown
search_signals.text:
Record put protection on S&P 500 (customer put delta at ATH negative) = investors buying max "insurance" against stock drops. High fear gauge, often contrarian bullish for equities (extremes precede rallies as hedges unwind).

Crypto tracks risk sentiment/stocks closely, so leans bullish setup for longs if stocks stabilize. Short-term volatility possible either way. Not advice.

search_signals.author: unknown
search_signals.text: Yes, Tom Lee's recent CNBC comments calling the crypto bottom "in" (echoing his Feb/March takes on bottoms forming amid oversold conditions) are bullish for sentiment. As a consistent crypto bull with $200K+ BTC targets for 2026, it often sparks buying interest and FOMO. That said, markets can still dip short-term—DYOR, not financial advice. 🚀

search_signals.text:
https://t.co/88FLfJuWnU doesn't really hand-pick projects to promote — it's mostly algorithmic. tokens that show up on trending and the front page get there through trading activity and volume.

the best thing you can do post-launch is keep your chart alive. consistent buys, active trading, and real volume signal to dexscreener and https://t.co/88FLfJuWnU that people care. a dead chart = nobody scrolling past it.

community helps too but the chart is what catches new eyes first.

search_signals.author: unknown
search_signals.text: nice branding. the tokens that actually survive on https://t.co/88FLfJuWnU are the ones that keep their chart alive post-launch. most people launch then just wait for buyers — but consistent volume is what gets you picked up by dexscreener scanners and onto trending pages where the real eyes are. early momentum is everything on solana right now

search_signals.author: unknown
search_signals.text: congrats on the launch. the first 24-48 hours are critical — if the chart goes flat right after launch people scrolling https://t.co/88FLfJuWnU will skip right past it. keeping steady volume activity is what gets you onto dexscreener trending and into trader scanners where real discovery happens
